mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodation zone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of 2017-07-15 indicates accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary accommodation cycle with rates below 2%
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is triggered when PAYEMS growth rate exceeds the 150k threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146,615k as of 2017-07-15 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations. The mechanism requires both series to show month-over-month growth simultaneously, indicating synchronized consumer and residential investment activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 478,873M and HOUST at 1,215k as of 2017-06-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in less than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint signal underperforms single-series RSAFS or HOUST signals by more than 200 basis points over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 105.0 index level signals supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.16 as of 2017-06-15 near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 105.0, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism activates when unemployment breaches the 4.5% threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of 2017-07-15 below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in less than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 245.0 index level combined with FEDFUNDS above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.048 and FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of 2017-07-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in less than 52% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis above 2.5% annualized rate signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is always-on for quarters meeting the growth threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,010.7B as of 2017-04-15 indicates expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in less than 58% of quarters when GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth rate decelerating below 1.0% month-over-month combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.4%) signals synchronized supply and labor market weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.16 and UNRATE at 4.3% as of 2017-06-15
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in less than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression versus additive single-series effects.
